name: Italian Social Republic (1943–1945)
description: A green, white and red vertical tricolour charged at the centre with a grey (silver) Roman eagle, wings displayed, gripping a gold fasces; the eagle is centred on the white band and overlaps into the green and red.
history: War flag of the Italian Social Republic (Repubblica Sociale Italiana, RSI), the German puppet state led by Benito Mussolini in German-occupied northern and central Italy from September 1943 until the Axis surrender in Italy in April–May 1945. The national flag was the plain tricolour without arms; the war flag added the eagle-and-fasces device. The eagle was variously rendered black in official descriptions and silver/grey in reproductions; the gold fasces evoked the symbol of Italian Fascism after which the regime modelled itself.

features.symbolism: the Roman eagle of the Fascist state grasping a gold fasces, the emblem of Italian Fascism
